compiled_state.title: As a developer, T9 kudos trigger fires when the project's configured linter produces zero violations
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace the Python-specific ruff-check implementation of T9 with a generic approach that reads commands.lint from dso-config.conf and runs it, awarding kudos when the configured linter exits 0.

**Why**: T9 today only awards kudos on Python projects with ruff. Any project that has a configured linter (ESLint, RuboCop, etc.) should receive the same recognition for keeping their codebase lint-clean.

**Scope**:
- IN: Read commands.lint from dso-config.conf; invoke it as a subprocess; exit 0 → award kudos; key absent → emit TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal; key present but non-zero exit → no award
- OUT: Defining commands.lint defaults (Epic B), any other trigger (T7, T12, T13, T4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, T9 fires and awards kudos on any project where commands.lint exits 0 (tested with a mocked config and subprocess)
  ← Satisfies: "T9 reads commands.lint (from Epic B); when present, fires on zero-violation result"
- When this story is complete, T9 emits a machine-readable TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal (not an error/exception) when commands.lint is absent from dso-config.conf
  ← Satisfies: "when absent: TRIGGER_SKIPPED"
- When this story is complete, the TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal fields conform to the schema established by Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) — no shared library imported from Kudos Epic 2
  ← Satisfies: "All five triggers emit machine-readable TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal conforming to the signal format established by Kudos Epic 2"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for T9 config-read logic, kudos-award path, and TRIGGER_SKIPPED path

## Considerations

- [Testing] Subprocess invocation of commands.lint must be mockable (use unittest.mock.patch on subprocess.run); do not depend on any real linter binary being present in the test environment
- [Maintainability] TRIGGER_SKIPPED signal format: reference Kudos Epic 2 (71df-5bd8) SC-E2 for the required field schema; implement independently (no import from Kudos Epic 2 modules)
- [Reliability] dispatch_kudos_trigger() from Kudos Epic 1 (3e9b-afee) must be present or mocked in tests — unit tests should mock this call

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
